---
title: "HW2"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Summary Statistics

## 2 quantitative variables, 2 ordinal variables, and 2 categorical variables

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Read the data
travel_data <- read.csv("travel_map.csv")

# Create ordered factors for ordinal variables
travel_data$Self_rated_health <- factor(travel_data$Self_rated_health, 
                                        levels = c("Poor", "Fair", "Good", "Very good", "Excellent"),
                                        ordered = TRUE)

travel_data$Household_income <- factor(travel_data$Household_income,
                                       levels = c("Under $10,000", "$10,000 to $34,999", 
                                                  "$35,000 to $74,999", "$75,000 to $149,999", 
                                                  "$150,000 and over"),
                                       ordered = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# 2 Quantitative Variables
summary(travel_data$Yearly_miles_personally_driven)

summary(travel_data$Avg_trip_distance_in_miles)

# 2 Ordinal Variables
table(travel_data$Self_rated_health)

table(travel_data$Household_income)

# 2 Categorical Variables
table(travel_data$Sex)

table(travel_data$Race)

```

```{r}
# Quantitative Variable 1: Yearly Miles Driven
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Yearly_miles_personally_driven)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Yearly Miles Driven",
       x = "Yearly Miles", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma)
```

```{r}
# Quantitative Variable 2: Average Trip Distance
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Avg_trip_distance_in_miles)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "coral", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Average Trip Distance",
       x = "Average Trip Distance (miles)", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma)
```

```{r}
# Ordinal Variable 1: Self-Rated Health
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Self_rated_health)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "forestgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Self-Rated Health",
       x = "Self-Rated Health", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
# Ordinal Variable 2: Household Income
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Household_income)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "goldenrod", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Household Income",
       x = "Household Income", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
# Categorical Variable 1: Sex
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Sex)) +
  geom_bar(fill = c("lightblue", "pink"), alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Sex",
       x = "Sex", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Categorical Variable 2: Race
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Race)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "purple", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Race",
       x = "Race", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```

```{r}
# Relationship 1: Income vs Yearly Miles
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Household_income, y = Yearly_miles_personally_driven)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Yearly Miles Driven by Income Level",
       x = "Household Income", y = "Yearly Miles") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma)



# Relationship 2: Health vs Trip Distance
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Self_rated_health, y = Avg_trip_distance_in_miles)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Average Trip Distance by Health Status",
       x = "Self-Rated Health", y = "Average Trip Distance") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma)



# Relationship 3: Age by Sex and Driver Status
ggplot(travel_data, aes(x = Sex, y = Age, fill = Driver_status)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution by Sex and Driver Status",
       x = "Sex", y = "Age", fill = "Driver Status") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue", "coral"))
```
